text: Blessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called children of God.
Blessed are those who dwell in your house; they are ever praising you.
Blessed are all who fear the Lord, who walk in obedience to him. You will eat the fruit of your labor; blessings and prosperity will be yours.
Taste and see that the Lord is good; blessed is the one who takes refuge in him.
Blessed are those who are persecuted because of righteousness, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
Blessed is the one who perseveres under trial because, having stood the test, that person will receive the crown of life that the Lord has promised to those who love him.
Whoever gives heed to instruction prospers, and blessed is the one who trusts in the Lord.
Blessed is the one whose transgressions are forgiven, whose sins are covered. Blessed is the one whose sin the Lord does not count against them and in whose spirit is no deceit.
Give, and it will be given to you. A good measure, pressed down, shaken together and running over, will be poured into your lap. For with the measure you use, it will be measured to you.
The blessing of the Lord brings wealth, without painful toil for it.
Bless those who persecute you; bless and do not curse.
But let him ask in faith, with no doubting, for the one who doubts is like a wave of the sea that is driven and tossed by the wind.
Jesus immediately reached out his hand and took hold of him, saying to him, ‘O you of little faith, why did you doubt?’

And Jesus answered them, ‘Truly, I say to you, if you have faith and do not doubt, you will not only do what has been done to the fig tree, but even if you say to this mountain, Be taken up and thrown into the sea, it will happen.’
Truly, I say to you, whoever says to this mountain, ‘Be taken up and thrown into the sea,’ and does not doubt in his heart, but believes that what he says will come to pass, it will be done for him.
Then he said to Thomas, ‘Put your finger here, and see my hands; and put out your hand, and place it in my side. Do not disbelieve, but believe.’
Commit your way to the Lord; trust in him, and he will act.
Take care, brothers, lest there be in any of you an evil, unbelieving heart, leading you to fall away from the living God.
When doubts filled my mind, your comfort gave me renewed hope and cheer. (NLT)
And he said to them, ‘Why are you troubled, and why do doubts arise in your hearts?’
’For I know the plans I have for you,’ declares the Lord, ‘plans to prosper you and not to harm you, plans to give you a hope and a future.’
Suppose one of you has a hundred sheep and loses one of them. Doesn’t he leave the ninety-nine in the open country and go after the lost sheep until he finds it?
Trust in the Lord with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding; in all your ways submit to him, and he will make your paths straight.
Your word is a lamp for my feet, a light on my path.
He refreshes my soul. He guides me along the right paths for his name’s sake.
I lift up my eyes to the mountains—where does my help come from? My help comes from the Lord, the Maker of heaven and earth.
In their hearts humans plan their course, but the Lord establishes their steps.
Whether you turn to the right or to the left, your ears will hear a voice behind you, saying, ‘This is the way; walk in it.’
I have strayed like a lost sheep. Seek your servant, for I have not forgotten your commands.
If I rise on the wings of the dawn, if I settle on the far side of the sea, even there your hand will guide me, your right hand will hold me fast.
You make known to me the path of life; in your presence there is fullness of joy; at your right hand are pleasures forevermore.
Do not be grieved, for the joy of the Lord is your strength.
This is the day that the Lord has made; let us rejoice and be glad in it.
Then our mouth was filled with laughter, and our tongue with shouts of joy; then they said among the nations, ‘The Lord has done great things for them.’
Rejoice always.
With joy you will draw water from the wells of salvation.
Be glad in the Lord, and rejoice, O righteous, and shout for joy, all you upright in heart!
I perceived that there is nothing better for them than to be joyful and to do good as long as they live.
The precepts of the Lord are right, rejoicing the heart; the commandment of the Lord is pure, enlightening the eyes.
A glad heart makes a cheerful face, but by sorrow of heart the spirit is crushed.
Cast all your anxiety on him because he cares for you.
Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat or drink; or about your body, what you will wear.
Can any one of you by worrying add a single hour to your life?
Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God.
And the peace of God, which transcends all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus.
So do not worry, saying, ‘What shall we eat?’ or ‘What shall we drink?’ or ‘What shall we wear?’
Look at the birds of the air; they do not sow or reap or store away in barns, and yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Are you not much more valuable than they?
Therefore do not worry about tomorrow, for tomorrow will worry about itself. Each day has enough trouble of its own.
In peace I will lie down and sleep, for you alone, Lord, make me dwell in safety.
When I am afraid, I put my trust in you.
Be strong and courageous. Do not be frightened, and do not be dismayed, for the Lord your God is with you wherever you go.
The Lord is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear?
I can do all things through Him who strengthens me.
When I am afraid, I put my trust in You.
God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble.
Do not be afraid of them; the Lord your God himself will fight for you.
The Lord is the strength of my life; of whom shall I be afraid?
Have I not commanded you? Be strong and courageous. Do not be frightened, and do not be dismayed, for the Lord your God is with you wherever you go.
The Lord is near to the brokenhearted and saves the crushed in spirit.
Trust in the Lord with all your heart, and do not lean on your own understanding.
You shall not take vengeance or bear a grudge against the sons of your own people, but you shall love your neighbor as yourself: I am the Lord.
Hatred stirs up strife, but love covers all offenses.
Whoever is slow to anger has great understanding, but he who has a hasty temper exalts folly.
A hot-tempered man stirs up strife, but he who is slow to anger quiets contention.
Good sense makes one slow to anger, and it is his glory to overlook an offense.
Be not quick in your spirit to become angry, for anger lodges in the bosom of fools.
Refrain from anger, and forsake wrath! Fret not yourself; it tends only to evil.
But I say to you that everyone who is angry with his brother will be liable to judgment...
For if you forgive others their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you, but if you do not forgive others their trespasses, neither will your Father forgive your trespasses.
And whenever you stand praying, forgive, if you have anything against anyone, so that your Father also who is in heaven may forgive you your trespasses.
Charm is deceitful, and beauty is vain, but a woman who fears the Lord is to be praised.
Man looks on the outward appearance, but the Lord looks on the heart.
Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you are like whitewashed tombs, which outwardly appear beautiful, but within are full of dead people’s bones.
Do not let your adorning be external… but let your adorning be the hidden person of the heart.
Vanity of vanities, says the Preacher, vanity of vanities! All is vanity.
Pride goes before destruction, and a haughty spirit before a fall.
Let us not become conceited, provoking one another, envying one another.
God opposes the proud but gives grace to the humble.
Do nothing from selfish ambition or conceit, but in humility count others more significant than yourselves.
Therefore let anyone who thinks that he stands take heed lest he fall.
Therefore welcome one another as Christ has welcomed you, for the glory of God.
My brothers, show no partiality as you hold the faith in our Lord Jesus Christ.
There is neither Jew nor Greek, there is neither slave nor free, there is no male and female, for you are all one in Christ Jesus.
Bear with one another and, if one has a complaint against another, forgive each other; as the Lord has forgiven you, so you also must forgive.
With all humility and gentleness, with patience, bearing with one another in love.
So whatever you wish that others would do to you, do also to them.
Finally, all of you, have unity of mind, sympathy, brotherly love, a tender heart, and a humble mind.
If possible, so far as it depends on you, live peaceably with all.
Do nothing from selfish ambition or conceit, but in humility count others more significant than yourselves.
For godly grief produces a repentance that leads to salvation without regret, whereas worldly grief produces death.
As far as the east is from the west, so far does he remove our transgressions from us.
Remember not the former things, nor consider the things of old. Behold, I am doing a new thing.
There is therefore now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.
Forget what lies behind and strain forward to what lies ahead.
The steadfast love of the Lord never ceases; his mercies never come to an end; they are new every morning.
If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive us and cleanse us from all unrighteousness.
Whoever conceals his transgressions will not prosper, but he who confesses and forsakes them will obtain mercy.
Those who look to him are radiant, and their faces shall never be ashamed.
Though your sins are like scarlet, they shall be as white as snow.
Fear not, for you will not be ashamed; be not confounded, for you will not be disgraced.
Casting all your anxieties on him, because he cares for you.
Indeed, none who wait for you shall be put to shame.
The fear of man lays a snare, but whoever trusts in the Lord is safe.
Let me not be put to shame, O Lord, for I have called upon you.
Neither death nor life… nor any other created thing, shall be able to separate us from the love of God.
Then I shall not be put to shame, having my eyes fixed on all your commandments.
My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.
Fear not, for I am with you; be not dismayed, for I am your God; I will strengthen you, I will help you, I will uphold you with my righteous right hand.
For God gave us a spirit not of fear but of power and love and self-control.
Peace I leave with you; my peace I give to you. Not as the world gives do I give to you.
Trust in the Lord with all your heart, and do not lean on your own understanding.
I sought the Lord, and he answered me and delivered me from all my fears.
Even though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil, for you are with me.
There is no fear in love, but perfect love casts out fear.
The end of the matter; all has been heard. Fear God and keep his commandments, for this is the whole duty of man.
For I know the plans I have for you, declares the Lord, plans for welfare and not for evil, to give you a future and a hope.
For my thoughts are not your thoughts, neither are your ways my ways, declares the Lord.
Whatever you do, work heartily, as for the Lord and not for men.
For to me to live is Christ, and to die is gain.
And we know that for those who love God all things work together for good.
You make known to me the path of life; in your presence there is fullness of joy.
Always give yourselves fully to the work of the Lord, for you know that your labor in the Lord is not in vain.
Fear not, for I have redeemed you; I have called you by name, you are mine.
If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive us our sins and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.
I acknowledged my sin to you, and I did not cover my iniquity; I said, ‘I will confess my transgressions to the Lord,’ and you forgave the iniquity of my sin.
If anyone is in Christ, he is a new creation. The old has passed away; behold, the new has come.
For I will be merciful toward their iniquities, and I will remember their sins no more.
Create in me a clean heart, O God, and renew a right spirit within me.
You, who were dead in your trespasses… God made alive together with him, having forgiven us all our trespasses, by canceling the record of debt that stood against us.
He will again have compassion on us; he will tread our iniquities underfoot and cast all our sins into the depths of the sea.
I can do all things through him who strengthens me.
In all your ways acknowledge him, and he will make straight your paths.
But they who wait for the Lord shall renew their strength; they shall mount up with wings like eagles.
The Lord is my strength and my shield; in him my heart trusts, and I am helped.
Commit your work to the Lord, and your plans will be established.
Do not be slothful in zeal, be fervent in spirit, serve the Lord.
Come to me, all who labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you, and learn from me… For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.
He makes me lie down in green pastures. He leads me beside still waters. He restores my soul.
It is in vain that you rise up early and go late to rest, eating the bread of anxious toil; for he gives to his beloved sleep.
He lay down under a broom tree and fell asleep. And behold, an angel touched him and said, ‘Arise and eat.’
My presence will go with you, and I will give you rest.
If you lie down, you will not be afraid; when you lie down, your sleep will be sweet.
I lift up my eyes to the hills. From where does my help come? My help comes from the Lord, who made heaven and earth.
Do not be anxious about tomorrow, for tomorrow will be anxious for itself. Sufficient for the day is its own trouble.
He who dwells in the shelter of the Most High will abide in the shadow of the Almighty. I will say to the Lord, ‘My refuge and my fortress, my God, in whom I trust.’
Blessed are those who mourn, for they shall be comforted.
He will wipe away every tear from their eyes, and death shall be no more.
Let not your hearts be troubled. You believe in God; believe also in me… I go to prepare a place for you.
Blessed be the God of all comfort, who comforts us in all our affliction.
To grant to those who mourn in Zion—to give them a beautiful headdress instead of ashes.
He heals the brokenhearted and binds up their wounds.
For the Lord will not cast off forever, but though he cause grief, he will have compassion.
Nothing shall separate us from the love of God in Christ Jesus our Lord.
May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, so that by the power of the Holy Spirit you may abound in hope.
Why are you cast down, O my soul? Hope in God; for I shall again praise him.
The steadfast love of the Lord never ceases; his mercies never come to an end; they are new every morning.
Let us hold fast the confession of our hope without wavering.
Behold, the eye of the Lord is on those who fear him, on those who hope in his steadfast love.
God’s love has been poured into our hearts through the Holy Spirit who has been given to us.
According to his great mercy, he has caused us to be born again to a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ.
I wait for the Lord, my soul waits, and in his word I hope.
No temptation has overtaken you that is not common to man. God is faithful, and he will not let you be tempted beyond your ability, but with the temptation he will also provide the way of escape.
For freedom Christ has set us free; stand firm therefore, and do not submit again to a yoke of slavery.
Let not sin therefore reign in your mortal body… but present yourselves to God as those who have been brought from death to life.
A man without self-control is like a city broken into and left without walls.
Submit yourselves therefore to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you.
And do not get drunk with wine, for that is debauchery, but be filled with the Spirit.
Take care, and be on your guard against all covetousness, for one’s life does not consist in the abundance of his possessions.
For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evils.
Keep your life free from love of money, and be content with what you have.
Whoever is greedy for unjust gain troubles his own household.
Do not lay up for yourselves treasures on earth… but lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven.
A greedy man stirs up strife, but the one who trusts in the Lord will be enriched.
He who loves money will not be satisfied with money.
You cannot serve God and money.
Whoever trusts in his riches will fall, but the righteous will flourish like a green leaf.
If you would be perfect, go, sell what you possess and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; and come, follow me.
One’s pride will bring him low, but he who is lowly in spirit will obtain honor.
Clothe yourselves, all of you, with humility toward one another, for God opposes the proud.
For the Lord of hosts has a day against all that is proud and lofty.
When pride comes, then comes disgrace, but with the humble is wisdom.
For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, and he who humbles himself will be exalted.
Do not think of yourself more highly than you ought, but think with sober judgment.
He has told you, O man, what is good; and what does the Lord require of you but to do justice, and to love kindness, and to walk humbly with your God?
Judge not, that you be not judged.
Do not speak evil against one another, brothers. The one who speaks against a brother or judges his brother speaks evil.
Why do you pass judgment on your brother? Or you, why do you despise your brother?
For with the judgment you pronounce you will be judged, and with the measure you use it will be measured to you.
Judge not, and you will not be judged; condemn not, and you will not be condemned; forgive, and you will be forgiven.
Let him who is without sin among you be the first to throw a stone.
You, therefore, have no excuse, O man, every one of you who judges.
Every way of a man is right in his own eyes, but the Lord weighs the heart.
Blessed are the merciful, for they shall obtain mercy.
Therefore do not pronounce judgment before the time, before the Lord comes.
Now faith is the assurance of things hoped for, the conviction of things not seen.
Fear not, for I am with you; be not dismayed, for I am your God; I will strengthen you, I will help you.
My flesh and my heart may fail, but God is the strength of my heart and my portion forever.
For we walk by faith, not by sight.
I believe; help my unbelief!
So faith comes from hearing, and hearing through the word of Christ.
If you have faith as a grain of mustard seed, you will say to this mountain, ‘Move from here to there,’ and it will move.
Jesus said to Thomas… ‘Blessed are those who have not seen and yet have believed.’
Why are you cast down, O my soul, and why are you in turmoil within me? Hope in God; for I shall again praise him, my salvation.
But let him ask in faith, with no doubting, for the one who doubts is like a wave of the sea.
Be strong and courageous. Do not fear or be in dread of them, for it is the Lord your God who goes with you. He will not leave you or forsake you.
Father of the fatherless and protector of widows is God in his holy habitation. God settles the solitary in a home; he leads out the prisoners to prosperity.
I will not leave you as orphans; I will come to you.
Turn to me and be gracious to me, for I am lonely and afflicted.
I will never leave you nor forsake you.
When my father and my mother forsake me, the Lord will take me in.
I am with you always, to the end of the age.
Where shall I go from your Spirit? Or where shall I flee from your presence? If I ascend to heaven, you are there! … your right hand shall hold me.
Come to me, all who labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.
Why are you cast down, O my soul, and why are you in turmoil within me? Hope in God; for I shall again praise him, my salvation and my God.

Do not be anxious about anything, but in everything by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known to God. And the peace of God, which surpasses all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus.
Weeping may tarry for the night, but joy comes with the morning.
Be angry and do not sin; do not let the sun go down on your anger.
Know this, my beloved brothers: let every person be quick to hear, slow to speak, slow to anger.
A soft answer turns away wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger.
But now you must put them all away: anger, wrath, malice, slander, and obscene talk from your mouth.
A fool gives full vent to his spirit, but a wise man quietly holds it back.
But I say to you that everyone who is angry with his brother will be liable to judgment.
Be not quick in your spirit to become angry, for anger lodges in the heart of fools.
Whoever is slow to anger is better than the mighty, and he who rules his spirit than he who takes a city.
Beloved, never avenge yourselves, but leave it to the wrath of God.
A tranquil heart gives life to the flesh, but envy makes the bones rot.
For where jealousy and selfish ambition exist, there will be disorder and every vile practice.
Love is patient and kind; love does not envy or boast.
Then I saw that all toil and all skill in work come from a jealous neighbor.
Fret not because of evildoers, nor be envious of wrongdoers.
Let not your heart envy sinners, but continue in the fear of the Lord all the day.
Let us walk properly as in the daytime, not in orgies and drunkenness, not in sexual immorality and sensuality, not in quarreling and jealousy.
So put away all malice and all deceit and hypocrisy and envy and all slander.
Be not envious of evil men, nor desire to be with them.
